front: Film: young Apu (son of a temple priest) and his sister run through a field of pampas grass to see a passing train
Film: trilogy inspired by Vittorio de Sica's Bicycle Thieves; scored by Ravi Shankar
Film: climax cuts from distraught parents to a zoom on the young protagonist holding an umbrella in shock
Film: ends with a shot of the protagonist's back as he walks toward a train under a cloud, deliberately avoiding his eyes
back: Apur Sansar / The World of Apu (Satyajit Ray)

front: Film: a bored woman peers at her oblivious husband through opera glasses; the camera tracks to keep a close-up on the glasses as she runs
Film: two nobles become so obsessed with chess that they ignore the impending British annexation of their territory
Director: scouted locations for Jean Renoir's The River (1951); called a protege of Renoir
Director: created the detective Feluda (with companion Topshe) and scientist Professor Shonku in his fiction writing
Director: edited the children's magazine Sandesh; published nonsense poetry collection Today Bandha Ghorar Dim including a Bengali translation of 'Jabberwocky'
Director: wrote The Alien (about a friendly alien); Spielberg has been accused of plagiarizing it for E.T.
Director: Ganashatru (1990) was an adaptation of Ibsen's An Enemy of the People
Director: frequent cinematographer Subrata Mitra pioneered bounce lighting using light-colored reflective surfaces
